senses.definition: relating to a primate or to the primacy of a church authority (often used in ecclesiastical contexts) or to a primal position of importance.
examples: The primatial see holds a place of honor in the church hierarchy.
He spoke of primatial duties within the ceremonial rites.

senses.definition: plural of primatial; refers to things related to primatial matters or dividends of primacy within a given context.
examples: The primatials discussed the succession.
Their notes covered several primatials from the meeting.

senses.definition: relating to primates or to the process of priming; resembling or characteristic of primates.
examples: The primatic imagery in the text evokes ancient forest scenes.
A primatic mood can be observed in early scientific descriptions.

senses.definition: relating to primates or to the primacy of something; archaic or formal variant of primatial/primarily.
examples: In primatical terms, the study focuses on social hierarchy.
A primatical assertion guided the debate.

senses.definition: relating to primatology, the scientific study of primates; sometimes used as an adjective in scholarly contexts.
examples: She pursued primatological research in the rainforest.
Primatological findings shed light on social behavior.

senses.definition: plural of primatology; the science dealing with primates.
examples: They compared several primatologies in the symposium.
Primatologies offer insights into evolution.

senses.definition: a scientist who studies primates.
examples: The primatologist observed the troop from a safe distance.
Primatologists often work in the field to study behavior.

senses.definition: plural of primatologist; multiple scientists who study primates.
examples: The conference gathered several primatologists from around the world.
Primatologists shared field notes.

senses.definition: the scientific study of primates.
examples: Primatology unlocks mysteries of social behavior in monkeys.
She specializes in primatology and conservation.

senses.definition: the Italian word for spring; used in English to refer to the season or in phrases like 'Primavera' as a brand or title.
examples: The garden blooms in primavera.
They decorated the arch with primavera colors.

senses.definition: relating to spring (primavera); characteristic of spring.
examples: The primaveral breeze felt fresh and light.
Primaveral colors adorned the landscape.

senses.definition: plural of primavera; references to springs or spring seasons in Italian context.
examples: The gardens awoke in primaveras of color.

senses.definition: the most important or best part; a crucial moment; also a number that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself (in math).
examples: Safety is the prime concern.
The prime numbers are fundamental in mathematics.

senses.definition: first in importance; main; of the best quality.
examples: She gave prime example after prime example.
Prime beef is very tender.

senses.definition: to prepare or make ready; to prime a pump or weapon; to prepare in advance.
examples: Prime the pump before starting.
They primed the audience with a teaser.

senses.definition: prepared or ready; having been prepared in advance.
examples: The team was primed for a quick turnaround.
Her questions primed the discussion.

senses.definition: a decorative gilt that is of prime quality; used rarely as a compound or product name.
examples: The cabinet featured primegilt trim.

senses.definition: in a prime or foremost manner; first or best.
examples: The book is primely positioned as a foundational text.
She answered primely and clearly.

senses.definition: the quality of being prime; the state of being first in importance or quality.
examples: primeness of purpose helped the project succeed.

senses.definition: plural of primeness; multiple qualities or states of being prime.
examples: The primenesses of the design are evident.

senses.definition: a short, introductory book or lesson; a small tool used to prepare surfaces; a first coat of paint; or an item used as a starter.
examples: He studied the English primer for beginners.
The painter applied a primer before painting.

senses.definition: a small device containing a material that lights a fuse in explosive devices or fireworks.
examples: The soldier lit the rocket with a primer fuse.

senses.definition: a person who primes or a first draft event; a person who introduces or initiates.
examples: She is the primer of the group, setting the tone.

senses.definition: the first course in a traditional Spanish-speaking meal; in theater, means ‘a member who stands first’ or used as a title.
examples: They served sopa as the primero before the main dish.
El primero en salir fue el actor principal.

senses.definition: a small, often decorative peg or knob; rare usage in English, sometimes a misspelling of primerole.
examples: The antique cabinet has a delicate primerole detail.

senses.definition: plural of primero; first courses in Spanish meals or the early steps in a process.
examples: Los primeros fueron deliciosos.

senses.definition: plural of primer; books for beginners or tools used to prime.
examples: Kids read several readers or primers in class.
A primer coat is applied before painting.

senses.definition: a small bottle or container used to apply a primer or cosmetic product.
examples: She carried a makeup primer in her bag.

senses.definition: plural of prime; the most important things or people; or prime numbers in math.
examples: Family is her primes in life.
The list contains all the primes in mathematics.

senses.definition: third person singular of prime; to prepare or set up; to prime a pump or weapon.
examples: This routine primes the pump for a successful session.

senses.definition: the period in the evening when television viewing is at its highest; peak time.
examples: The show will air during primetime.
They scheduled the program for primetime.
